records.ety: [L. conspicuus, fr. conspicere to get sight of, to perceive; con- + spicere, specere, to look. See Spy]

defs: Open to the view; obvious to the eye; easy to be seen; plainly visible; manifest; attracting the eye.
quotes: It was a rock Of alabaster, piled up to the clouds, Conspicious far.
Conspicious by her veil and hood, Signing the cross, the abbess stood.

defs: Obvious to the mental eye; easily recognized; clearly defined; notable; prominent; eminent; distinguished; as, a conspicuous excellence, or fault.
quotes: A man who holds a conspicuous place in the political, ecclesiastical, and literary history of England.
